name: binlog-analyzer
description: Analyze MSBuild binary logs with Copilot Chat and MCP tools

viewsWelcome.contents:
No binlog loaded.

Load a binlog file to get started with build analysis.

[Load Binlog File](command:binlog.loadFile)

Or open a .binlog from Structured Log Viewer using ✨ Open in VS Code.

binlogAnalyzer.activeBinlogs.description: Binlog file paths to load automatically. Set by Structured Log Viewer when opening in VS Code.

binlogAnalyzer.mcpServerPath.description: Custom path to the baronfel.binlog.mcp dotnet tool. Leave empty to use the globally installed tool.

binlogAnalyzer.autoLoad.description: Automatically load binlog and push diagnostics when activated via URI handler.

binlogAnalyzer.diagnosticsSeverityFilter.description: Minimum severity level to show in the Problems panel.

binlogAnalyzer.inlineDecorations.description: Show build errors as inline decorations in source files.

binlogAnalyzer.redaction.autodetectCommo.description: Automatically detect common secret patterns (API keys, connection strings, tokens) during redaction.

binlogAnalyzer.redaction.autodetectUsern.description: Detect and redact username occurrences during redaction.

binlogAnalyzer.redaction.processEmbedded.description: Also redact secrets inside embedded source files in the binlog.

chatParticipants.name: binlog
chatParticipants.description: Ask questions about MSBuild binary logs — build errors, performance, targets, and more.

commands.name: errors
commands.description: Show build errors and warnings with analysis

commands.name: secrets
commands.description: Scan for leaked secrets, tokens, and credentials in the binlog

commands.name: compare
commands.description: Compare two loaded binlogs to spot regressions and configuration differences

chatModes.name: Build Analysis
chatModes.description: Pre-configured for MSBuild binlog investigation with binlog MCP tools.
chatModes.systemPrompt:
You are an MSBuild build analysis expert. You have access to binlog MCP tools.

IMPORTANT: Call load_binlog ONLY ONCE per conversation. If you already called it, skip straight to analysis tools. Check copilot-instructions.md for the full absolute binlog path.

STEP 1 (first message only): Call load_binlog with the FULL ABSOLUTE PATH. NEVER use a relative filename.
STEP 2: Call analysis tools (get_diagnostics, list_projects, etc.) with binlog_file set to the same full absolute path.

All tools require 'binlog_file' parameter with the FULL ABSOLUTE PATH. Ask the user for the path if you don't know it.

Always reference specific file paths and line numbers when discussing errors.
